name: Valle de Aisa – Pico Aspe desde La Cleta
name_original: Valle de Aísa – Pico Aspe desde La Cleta
region_intro: Hiking around Aísa offers diverse landscapes within the Spanish Pyrenees, characterized by significant elevation changes and dramatic mountain scenery. The region features high peaks, verdant valleys, and varied pre-Pyrenean terrain. This mountainous environment provides a range of hiking experiences, from accessible paths to demanding multi-day treks. Best hiking trails around Aísa The most popular hiking route is Valle de Aisa – Pico Aspe desde La Cleta, a 6.0 miles (9.7 km) difficult trail that takes 4 hours 39 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation gain towards Pico Aspe. Another top favourite among local hikers is Pre-Pyrenean Trail (GR 15): Aísa to Jasa, a moderate 6.9 miles (11.2 km) path. This section of the GR 15 offers diverse landscapes and connects Aísa to Jasa. Local hikers also love the Las Llanas – Pico Aspe loop from La Cleta, a 7.7 miles (12.4 km) trail leading through challenging Pyrenean terrain, often completed in about 5 hours 26 minutes. Hiking around Aísa is defined by Pyrenean peaks, verdant valleys, and impressive elevation changes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to demanding multi-day treks. The routes in Aísa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 60 reviews. More than 800 hikers have used komoot to explore Aísa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Aísa?
A: Aísa offers a diverse range of hiking experiences with over 30 routes. These trails cater to various fitness levels, from easy walks to challenging multi-day treks through the Pyrenees.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Aísa?
A: Yes, Aísa has several easy trails perfect for beginners or families. While the region is known for its mountainous terrain, there are at least 3 easy routes available. These paths allow you to enjoy the beautiful Pyrenean scenery without significant elevation challenges.

Q: What kind of challenging hikes can I find in Aísa?
A: For experienced hikers, Aísa provides numerous challenging routes, with 11 difficult trails featuring significant elevation gain and rugged Pyrenean terrain. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e933149097/valle-de-aisa-pico-aspe-desde-la-cleta" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valle de Aisa – Pico Aspe desde La Cleta&lt;/a&gt;, a 9.7 km route with over 1000 meters of ascent towards Pico Aspe.

Q: Are there any circular walks in Aísa?
A: Yes, Aísa offers several circular walks. One popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15009502" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Llanas – Pico Aspe loop from La Cleta&lt;/a&gt;, a 12.4 km trail that takes you through demanding Pyrenean landscapes, typically completed in about 5 hours 26 minutes.

Q: Can I hike sections of long-distance trails near Aísa?
A: Absolutely. Aísa is a key point for several renowned long-distance trails. You can hike a moderate 11.2 km section of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e944629019/pre-pyrenean-trail-gr-15-aisa-to-jasa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pre-Pyrenean Trail (GR 15) from Aísa to Jasa&lt;/a&gt;, offering diverse landscapes and village charm. The GR 11 (Senda Pirenaica) and Haute Route Pyrénéenne (HRP) also pass through or near the region.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I expect to see on hikes around Aísa?
A: Hiking around Aísa offers stunning natural features. You can encounter snow-capped peaks, verdant valleys, and impressive elevation changes characteristic of the Pyrenees. Notable natural monuments nearby include the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/571806" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Anayet Lakes&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5586805" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estanés Lake&lt;/a&gt;, as well as prominent summits like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5393796" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pic des Moines&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical sites or points of interest along the trails?
A: While the focus is on natural beauty, the broader Aragon area, including Aísa, has historical elements. The region research mentions ancient structures like the 11th-century Torre de Tou near Aínsa, adding a historical dimension to the hiking experience. You might also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24665970" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen de Izagra loop from La Cleta&lt;/a&gt;, which hints at ancient human presence.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Aísa?
A: The Pyrenean region around Aísa offers diverse conditions throughout the year. While specific best times depend on your preferred activity and trail difficulty, generally, late spring to early autumn provides the most favorable weather for hiking, with accessible trails and pleasant temperatures. High-altitude routes are best avoided in winter unless you are equipped for snow and ice.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Aísa?
A: The hiking routes in Aísa are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an impressive average score of 4.6 stars from over 60 reviews. Hikers often praise the dramatic mountain scenery, the diverse landscapes, and the variety of options available for different skill levels.

Q: Are there any routes that cross into France from Aísa?
A: Yes, given Aísa's location in the Pyrenees, some routes can lead towards or cross the border into France. An example is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20524117" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Crossing of the Gave d'Aspe loop from Somport&lt;/a&gt;, a 16.7 km route that explores the border region and offers a taste of both Spanish and French Pyrenean landscapes.

Q: Are there mountain huts or refuges available for multi-day hikes?
A: Yes, for those undertaking longer or multi-day hikes, there are several mountain huts and refuges in the vicinity of Aísa. Notable options include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/565433" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Refuge d'Ayous&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2234384" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Refuge de Pombie&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4414444" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lizara Refuge&lt;/a&gt;, providing essential shelter and services for hikers.
tips: This 2645 m high peak is one of the most legendary and recognizable in the area.
Despite its name, this is a terrain where one new slope after another continually appears.

waypoints.name: Riachuelo Crossing in the Valles Occidentales Natural Park
